mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the equity risk premium discount rate, sustaining a persistent tailwind for equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns should skew positive on average relative to unconditional baseline.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during FEDFUNDS<0.5% periods is not statistically greater than the full-sample mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, causing equity markets to price in earnings risk and produce negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the signal shows no significant difference from unconditional 60-day return distribution (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal weakening consumer demand and construction activity, a combined downturn in both discretionary spending and durable-goods-linked investment that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline AND HOUST MoM decline concurrently
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=439771 and HOUST=1089 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes fail to produce negative mean 20-day forward returns distinct from single-series decline episodes (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion regime, positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects rising industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which propagates quickly into short-horizon equity price appreciation over the following trading week.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive industrial output growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on positive INDPRO MoM growth are not significantly different from returns conditional on negative growth (t-test p>0.05) within expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects ongoing labor market expansion and income generation supporting consumer spending, which continuously feeds into positive equity market sentiment and forward returns over a monthly horizon, independent of any specific threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or sign concordance rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 3%, markets anticipate an accelerated Federal Reserve tightening cycle, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as rate-hike expectations are priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=236.149 as of 2014-12, YoY well below 3% currently
failure_condition: Triggered episodes with CPI YoY>3% show non-negative mean 60-day forward returns, or difference from baseline is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth (even while still positive) signals diminishing marginal economic momentum, prompting a repricing of forward equity risk premia and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors anticipate slower earnings growth.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating GDP growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth deceleration are not significantly negative relative to acceleration periods (p>0.10), or effect reverses sign in more than one out of three historical late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of falling UNRATE toward levels consistent with full employment combined with a still-low FEDFUNDS rate creates a policy-lag mismatch: markets interpret tightening labor slack as a precursor to imminent rate hikes, producing short-horizon volatility and negative equity returns as the interaction between labor tightness and monetary stance (not either series alone) drives repricing.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.7 and FEDFUNDS=0.11 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint low-UNRATE/low-FEDFUNDS episodes are not significantly different from returns when only one condition holds (interaction coefficient p>0.10 in regression), or mean return is not negative.
